notes: The Philosophy major covers a wide variety of topics including philosophy of language, philosophy of mind, metaphysics, philosophy of science, logic, philosophy of mathematics, ethics, social and political philosophy, philosophy of law, and the history of philosophy, from antiquity to the present. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. To be considered for this major, you must complete the following courses by the spring before transfer: One introductory course in Philosophy of the mind or in skepticism and rationality, One introductory course in Ethical Theory, One introductory course in Symbolic Logic, and One additional introductory philosophy course (i. e. contemporary moral issues, Western Philosophy etc). All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://philosophy .ucla.edu and https:// admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
